title: Trump tells allies to reopen Strait of Hormuz alone | 7NEWS
description: Donald Trump has told allied countries to 'get their own oil' and reopen the Strait of Hormuz themselves, expressing frustration that European nations and other allies are refusing to join a conflict with Iran. The US President posted on Truth Social telling allies to 'build up some delayed courage' and take the Strait, stating America won't help them anymore. Iran's Revolutionary Guard is now threatening to target American tech companies including Google, Meta and Apple operating in the Middle East. title: Sydney shooting, Trump's NATO ultimatum and Freeman manhunt details | 7NEWS
description: A man is under police guard in hospital after a shooting at a Western Sydney home overnight, with a 33-year-old suffering a serious gunshot wound to his stomach. Donald Trump has criticised NATO allies, demanding they secure the Strait of Hormuz themselves and retrieve their own oil as fighting in Iran continues into its second month. New details emerge about the police hunt for Desi Freeman, including an intercepted phone call and the final confrontation where police fired 20 bullets after he emerged from his hideout. title: Trump tells the world to ‘get your own oil’ in furious rant
description: US President Donald Trump has unleashed on his NATO allies this morning telling them it’s time to “go get their own oil.”  Posting on Truth social late on Tuesday night Trump said countries struggling with oil supply should either buy it from the US or go to the Strait of Hormuz and “take it”.  “All of those countries that can’t get jet fuel because of the Strait of Hormuz, like the United Kingdom, which refused to get involved in the decapitation of Iran, I have a suggestion for you: Number 1, buy from the US, we have plenty, and Number 2, build up some delayed courage, go to the Strait, and just TAKE IT,” he wrote.  “You’ll have to start learning how to fight for yourself, the USA. won’t be there to help you anymore, just like you weren’t there for us. Iran has been, essentially, decimated. The hard part is done. Go get your own oil!” #oil #trump #hormuz #iran #middleeast

title: Government's fuel excise comes into effect
description: From today, the government has officially cut the fuel excise in half knocking 26 cents off a litre of petrol.  But Aussies are being told not to expect instant relief with prices at the pump needing time to catch up.  Though it’s welcome news for motorists, some economists warn this relief could keep inflation higher for longer. #fuel #oil #fuelexcise #petrol #diesel

description: A 33-year-old man is under police guard in hospital after being shot in the stomach in western Sydney. #crime #police #investigation #westernsydney #greystanes

title: Massive haul of allegedly stolen Lego seized in Adelaide raid | 7NEWS
description: South Australia Police conducted a major raid on a home in Adelaide's western suburbs, seizing what is alleged to be a massive haul of stolen Lego products. The operation extended past midnight, with police horse floats used to remove the extensive collection of boxed Lego sets that filled the property. A resident was arrested and refused police bail, with court proceedings scheduled to follow. title: NASA counts down to first moon mission in 53 years | 7NEWS
description: NASA has begun the countdown for the Artemis II mission, humanity's first crewed launch to the moon in 53 years, scheduled for tomorrow morning. The mission aims to establish a lunar outpost that will eventually serve as a launch pad for future Mars missions. Weather conditions remain the primary concern, with four launch attempts available over six days, though officials express optimism about the first launch window. title: Sydney and Melbourne house prices fall again | 7NEWS
description: House prices in Sydney and Melbourne have declined for the second consecutive month, with Sydney values dropping 0.3% and Melbourne falling 0.6% in March. Rising interest rates, inflation and global tensions are deterring buyers from the property market. Population growth in both cities has slowed to its lowest level since the pandemic, contributing to the downward pressure on housing values. title: NDIS crackdown and Gen Z's return to old-school hobbies | 7NEWS
description: The Australian Senate has passed new laws imposing tougher penalties on fraudulent NDIS operators, with offenders facing up to five years imprisonment and fines of $400,000. New research reveals three-quarters of Australians favour sports uniforms over formal school uniforms for students. A recent study shows Gen Z is abandoning digital entertainment in favour of traditional hobbies including scrapbooking, vinyl record collecting, and crafts like crocheting and knitting. title: Police identify Dezi Freeman's body after cop killer shot dead in Thologolong, Victoria
description: A coroner has formally identified the body of cop killer Dezi Freeman after he was shot around 20 times by highly trained police in Thologolong, Victoria, ending a three-hour stand-off. #dezifreeman #vicpolice #police #victoria #crime
